subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Buildings
- **1** main game board
- **44** building cards (various VP values and resource costs)
- **1** cathedral board (shared construction project)

#### Worker Pieces
- **20 workers** per player (in 5 player colors)
- **1** score marker per player

#### Resources & Currency
- **Wood tokens** — basic building resource
- **Stone tokens** — advanced building resource
- **Clay tokens** — common building resource
- **Gold tokens** — premium resource and currency
- **Marble tokens** — used for cathedral contributions
- **Silver coins** — currency for various actions

#### Cards & Tracks
- **Apprentice cards** — provide bonus abilities
- **Virtue track markers** — 1 per player
- **Debt cards** — negative VP if accumulated
- **Black market cards** — provide resources at virtue cost
- **Tax stand** — collects captured workers

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Place the main board** — Center of the table.
2. **Location spaces** — The board shows multiple action spaces: Quarry, Forest, Mines, Silversmith, Workshop, Guild Hall, Town Centre, King's Storehouse, Guardhouse, Black Market, Tax Stand.
3. **Building card display** — Lay out a market row of building cards.
4. **Cathedral board** — Place beside the main board with contribution spaces.
5. **Virtue track** — Located on the board, place all virtue markers at the center starting position (7).
6. **Resource supply** — Organize wood, stone, clay, gold, marble, and silver near the board.
7. **Debt cards** — Place in a supply pile.
8. **Black market deck** — Shuffle and place face-down.
9. **Apprentice deck** — Shuffle and place face-down.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Receives
- **20 workers** in their chosen color
- **1 score marker** — place on 0 of VP track
- **1 virtue marker** — place on space 7 of the virtue track
- **Starting resources** — a small amount of silver and one building card

#### Apprentice Cards
- Each player starts with **1 apprentice** card that provides a special ongoing ability.

#### Turn Order
- Determine randomly. Play proceeds clockwise.
- No compensation for later positions.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **All workers in hand** — You start with all 20 workers available.
- **Virtue at 7** — Everyone begins at a neutral moral standing.
- **No debt** — No player starts with debt cards.
- **Building market** — A row of building cards is available for all to see and plan around.
- **The game has no fixed rounds** — play continues until the endgame trigger.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, you perform **one action** by placing **one worker** on a location:

1. **Choose a location** on the board.
2. **Place one worker** from your supply onto that location.
3. **Resolve the action** — the more of YOUR workers already on that space, the stronger the action.

#### Stacking Workers
- The key mechanic: placing your 1st worker on the Quarry gives you 1 stone. Your 2nd worker on the same Quarry gives you 2 stone. Your 3rd gives 3, and so on.
- This means concentrating workers on one space gives escalating returns.
- But it makes you vulnerable to **capture**.

#### Alternative: Capture Workers
- Instead of placing a worker on a resource space, you may go to the **Town Centre** and capture ALL of one opponent's workers from a single location.
- Captured workers go to the **Tax Stand** (jail).
- The capturing player gains 1 silver per captured worker.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Resource Locations
- **Quarry** — Gain stone (1 per your worker here).
- **Forest** — Gain wood (1 per your worker here).
- **Mines** — Gain clay or gold.
- **Silversmith** — Gain silver coins.

#### Building & Crafting
- **Workshop** — Build a building card from your hand by paying its resource cost. Gain VP shown on card.
- **Guild Hall** — Take a building card from the market into your hand.

#### Special Locations
- **King's Storehouse** — Contribute marble to the cathedral. Gain VP for contributions.
- **Town Centre** — Capture an opponent's group of workers from one space.
- **Guardhouse** — Send captured workers to jail and gain silver.
- **Tax Stand** — Pay silver to retrieve your own workers from jail.
- **Black Market** — Gain resources at reduced cost but **lose virtue**.

#### Apprentices
- **Hire apprentices** from the available display to gain ongoing abilities.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Virtue Track
- Ranges from **0 (corrupt) to 14 (virtuous)**.
- Using the **Black Market** lowers your virtue.
- Certain actions and buildings **raise** your virtue.
- At game end, your position on the virtue track scores or costs VP.
- Very low virtue gives **negative points**; high virtue gives **bonus points**.

#### Capturing & Jail
- When your workers are captured, they go to the Tax Stand.
- You must spend a turn and pay silver to **bail them out**.
- While in jail, those workers are unavailable to you.
- Capturing is both offensive (deny opponent their stacked benefit) and economic (gain silver).

#### Debt Cards
- Certain actions or running out of silver may force you to take **debt cards**.
- Each debt card is worth **-2 VP** at end of game.
- You can pay off debt during the game by spending silver.

#### Cathedral Contributions
- Contributing marble to the cathedral earns VP.
- The cathedral has limited contribution spaces, creating a race.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Triggering the End
The game ends immediately when **one of these occurs**:
- The **cathedral** is fully constructed (all contribution spaces filled).
- A certain number of **building cards** have been built (varies by player count).
- The **debt card supply** runs out.

#### Final Scoring
- **Buildings** — VP printed on each building you constructed.
- **Cathedral contributions** — VP for marble contributed to the cathedral.
- **Virtue track** — VP bonus or penalty based on your position.
- **Debt** — Each debt card is **-2 VP**.
- **Gold** — Leftover gold may convert to VP.
- **Apprentice bonuses** — Some apprentices provide end-game VP.

#### Tiebreaker
Player with more remaining silver wins ties.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Stack early for efficiency** — Place multiple workers on one resource space to get large payoffs before opponents capture them.
- **Grab building cards** — Visit the Guild Hall early to get high-value buildings before they're taken.
- **Watch your virtue** — Decide early if you're going virtuous (bonus VP) or corrupt (black market efficiency).
- **Don't neglect silver** — You need it to bail workers out of jail and pay costs.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Over-stacking without building** — Gathering resources is pointless if you don't build. Convert resources to buildings.
- **Ignoring the capture threat** — If you stack 5+ workers on one space, that's a juicy capture target. Diversify or build quickly.
- **Accumulating too much debt** — At -2 VP each, debt cards add up fast. Pay them off when possible.
- **Neglecting the cathedral** — Cathedral contributions are efficient VP. Don't let opponents fill all spaces.
- **Virtue extremes without commitment** — Being in the middle of the virtue track scores nothing. Commit to high or manage low carefully.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Virtuous vs. corrupt path** — High virtue scores bonus VP but limits resource options. Low virtue gives cheap resources but costs VP if not managed.
- **When to capture** — Timing captures to maximize your silver gain and disrupt opponents at critical moments.
- **Cathedral vs. buildings** — Both score VP but require different resources and timing.
- **Worker management** — Knowing when to bail workers from jail vs. working with fewer workers.
- **Building selection** — Choosing buildings that match your available resources and strategy.
